Berberis is a plant with a definition that can be described as a deciduous shrub with a woody stem and compound leaves that are typically dark green in color. 

The health benefits of Berberis include its potential to act as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, and it may help to support liver and cardiovascular health. 

The bioactive constituents of Berberis include alkaloids such as berberine, berbamine, and oxyacanthine, which are known for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. 

The most common herbal preparations made with Berberis include capsules, tablets, and teas, which are often used to support digestive health and immune function.
